description_long: This object is a corner section of a stone block barrier, featuring large, rectangular stone blocks stacked in two perpendicular directions to form an L-shaped structure. The blocks have a weathered, slightly stained surface with visible horizontal seams and some discoloration, suggesting exposure to the elements. The stone faces are flat but show subtle textural irregularities, and the edges are mostly straight but not perfectly sharp, indicating some wear. The base of the structure is wider, possibly for stability, and the upper blocks are slightly recessed. The coloration is predominantly gray with hints of white and darker streaks, likely from water runoff or pollution. This type of structure is commonly found as part of urban retaining walls, building foundations, or decorative barriers in city environments.
description: A weathered, L-shaped stone block barrier with stacked rectangular blocks, gray coloration, and visible surface stains. The structure appears robust and is typical of urban retaining walls or building corners.
description_view_0: The corner of the stone block barrier is prominent, showing the intersection of two walls and the layered block construction.
description_view_1: A side view highlighting the vertical stacking of the stone blocks and the depth of the structure.
description_view_2: An interior angle view revealing the L-shaped configuration and the alignment of the blocks along both walls.
description_view_3: Another side view, similar to view 1, but from the opposite direction, showing the block layers and the base's width.
attribute_location_context: urban retaining wall or building foundation corner
attribute_installation_method: mortared and set on a stable base
attribute_weathering_pattern: Visible surface staining and discoloration, likely from water runoff and urban pollution. Edges show minor wear but no major chipping.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: forms part of a continuous wall
